obituary_0.name: Doris Holland Bradshaw
obituary_0.obituary_text: Doris Holland BradshawMay 28, 1935 - April 3, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Doris BradshawMay 28, 1935 ~ April 3, 2025Doris Bradshaw, age 89, passed away on April 3, 2025, in a McGregor Retirement Center.Visitation will be held from 10 to 12 noon on Wednesday, April 9, 2025, at the Valley Mills Church of Christ.   Graveside will be later at 2 P.M. Wednesday at Rosemound Cemetery in Waco, Tx. under the directions of Foss Funeral Home & Cremation Center.In lieu of flowers memorials may be made to Valley Mills Church of Christ.Doris was born on May 28, 1935, in Cameron, Texas, to Lewis and Beulah Holland. The family later moved to Waco, Texas, where she attended school and graduated from Waco High School, in 1953. She later married Walter Bradshaw on November 24, 1954. She and Walter always remembered each others birthday. They both were born on  May 28th. Not only did they shared a birthday but they also share all of the work taking care of the family farm, raising crops and cattle. After their children were born, David and Ruth Ann, she was devoted to them and all of their school activities. Doris later graduated from be a mother to a grandmother and great grandmother. She was very proud of her family. Talking about them always made her smile.Doris was a member of the Valley Mills Church of Christ. She loved the people there and attended until her health no longer permitted.She was preceded in death by her parents Lewis and Beulah Holland, husband Walter Bradshaw, 2 brothers:  Hubert and Robert Holland.Survivors include her children: David Bradshaw, Ruth Bradshaw Paez and her husband Johnny, a brother: Tom Holland and sister-in-law Linda; grandchildren: Stephanie McCormack and husband Zachary, Ranessa Grant, Cordell Grant and fiancée Madilyn Ploutz-Caulfield, April Reel Bogus and husband Darren; great grandchildren: Sage McCormack, John Bogus, and Dorothy Bogus.Doris Bradshaw Memorial VideoPlease join us in remembering Doris by signing our guestbook atfossfunealhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofDoris Holland Bradshaw, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofDoris Holland Bradshaw, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.SERVICESVisitationWednesday, April 09, 202510:00 AM - 12:00 PMGravesideWednesday, April 09, 20252:00 PMRosemount CemeteryWaco, TX 76710Get Directions on Google MapsPrint ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_1.name: Diane Jeanette Jordan
obituary_1.obituary_text: Diane Jeanette JordanJanuary 25, 1945 - April 3, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Diane Jeanette (Freedman) JordanJanuary 25, 1945-April 3, 2025Diane Jeanette(Freedman) Jordan, known lovingly by her family as Chinamo, passed away on Thursday April 3,2025 in Waco, Texas after a brief illness. Diane was born on January 25, 1945, in Valley Mills, Tx. to Emily (York) and Jesse Freedman.Diane graduated from Valley Mills High School in 1964 and attended 4-C Business College. Diane worked for various companies before retiring from the McLennan County Sheriff’s Office. In 1972 , Diane met and married Paul Jordan and lived in Waco, Tx. Diane and Paul later moved to Irving, then Duncanville until Paul’s death in 1983. After Paul’s death, Diane returned to the central Texas, and lived in Temple, Tx at the time of her death.Diane was the life of the party, a notorious flirt, and loved to go country dancing with her many friends. Diane adored her friend and family, loved to shop, garden, make homemade candy, play bingo, Dr Pepper and always on the go. Diane was a die-hard Valley Mills Eagle fan and could be seen at multiple sporting events cheering on her nieces and nephews (both biologic and those she adopted as her own). When her great-nephew became a coach, she became a huge fan of the Santo Wildcats. Diane loved to spoil her nieces and nephews and spend time with her family, especially the “Mann Clan”.Diane was preceded in death by her husband Paul, her parents Emily and Jesse Freedman, her siblings: Lilly Mae Freedman Light, Corene Freedman Riddle, Jesse(Ned) Freedman, Wiiliam (Gene) Freedman, Ray Freedman, Velma Freedman Johnson, D.G. Freedman, Richard Freedman, Duncan Freedman and Inez Freedman Rogers. She is survived by her sister Delma Freedman Mann and husband Sammy of Valley Mills, her brother Carroll Freedman of Valley Mills and Harry Freedman and wife Kristy of China Springs, and her many beloved nieces and nephews. She is also survived by her precious puppies Barkley and Bubba, her dear friend and companion Otis Ferguson and numerous other amazing friends and family.Chinamo was truly one of a kind and left a lasting impression on everyone she met. She will be missed by everyone who knew and loved her.Pallbearers include Adam Mann, Devon Mann, Manny Perez, Mike Chrisman, Wesley McDaniel and Thomas Perez.Honorary Pallbearers Tammie Mann-Chrisman, Necole Mann McDaniel, Kelby Mann Perez, AvaGrace Warner, Katie Ortiz and Lexie Ortiz.Memorials may be made to the Valley Mills Youth Baseball Association, PO Box 234, Valley Mills, Tx. 76689Diane Jordan Memorial VideoVisitationMonday, April 7, 2025, 5:00 -7:00 PMFoss Funeral Home & Cremation Center600 Ave. C, Valley Mills, Texas 76689Funeral ServiceTuesday, April 8, 2025. 1:00 PMFirst Baptist Church302 Ave. C Valley Mills, TexasIntermentChina Spring CemeteryPlease join us in remembering Diane by signing our guestbook atfossfuneralhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofDiane Jeanette Jordan, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofDiane Jeanette Jordan, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.SERVICESVisitationMonday, April 07, 2025Foss Funeral Home & Cremation Center600 Ave. CValley Mills, Texas 76689Get Directions on Google MapsFuneral ServiceTuesday, April 08, 20251:00 PMFirst Baptist Church302 Ave. CValley Mills, TexasGet Directions on Google MapsPrint ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_2.name: Danny Joe Kimberley
obituary_2.obituary_text: Danny Joe KimberleyJune 30, 1947 - June 6, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Danny Joe KimberleyJune 30, 1947 – June 6, 2025Danny Joe Kimberley, 77, of Valley Mills, Texas, passed away peacefully surrounded by family on June 6, 2025.Born on June 30, 1947, in State Center, Iowa, to Howard and Barbara Kimberley, Danny led a life marked by service, devotion, and quiet strength. Danny honorably served his country in the United States Navy during the Vietnam War, working as a Sonar Technician aboard the USS Cone. His commitment to duty continued beyond his military service as he spent 33 dedicated years with MidAmerica Energy, where he was respected by colleagues and friends alike.After retiring, Danny remained as active and dedicated as ever. He lent his skills to numerous building projects at his church, helped train miniature horses, and took great pride in maintaining the family homestead—keeping the grounds so immaculate they looked like a golf course.Danny is survived by his beloved wife, Patricia, and her children: Candice Clemmons of Round Rock, Texas; Brittany Jendel (Chase) of Lake Jackson, Texas; and Garrett Hendrix of Charlottesville, Virginia. He is also survived by his son Chad Kimberley (Tammy) and grandchildren Bri Reneau (Josh), Rachel Else (Aiden), and Jon Kimberley all of Washington as well as his daughter, Nicole Kimberley-Staeheli, and grandchildren Anna, Grace, and Evan Staeheli all of Minnesota. Though he deeply loved his entire family, the apple of his eye was his treasured great-granddaughter, Adriana Senarighi-Staeheli, who held a special place in his heart.A graveside ceremony will be Tuesday, June 17th at the Central Texas Veterans Cemetery, where Danny will be laid to rest with the honors he earned and deserved.A celebration of Danny’s life will be held at 11:30 AM at Top Hand Cowboy Church, with Pastor Greg Moore officiating.In lieu of flowers, the family requests that donations be made to Top Hand Cowboy Church, the Alzheimer’s Association, or a veterans organization of your choice.Danny will be remembered for his steadfast character, generous spirit, and enduring love for his family. He leaves behind a legacy of service, strength, and unconditional love.Danny Joe Kimberley Memorial VideoPlease join us in remembering Danny by signing our guestbook atfossfunealhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofDanny Joe Kimberley, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofDanny Joe Kimberley, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.SERVICESCelebration of lifeTuesday, June 17, 202511:30 AMTop Hand Cowboy Church,Get Directions on Google MapsPrint ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_3.name: Maria Lourdes Cardona
obituary_3.obituary_text: Maria Lourdes CardonaOctober 21, 1966 - July 24, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Maria CardonaOctober 21, 1966 ~ July 24, 2025Maria Lourdes Cardona, age 58, died peacefully and home in Valley Mills, Texas, on July 24, 2025, after a courageous battle with cancer.A funeral service will be held at 11 AM Tuesday, July 29, 2025, at the Valley Mills First Baptist Church.Visitation will be held one hour prior to the service at the church. A private visitation for family only will be held on Monday at Foss Funeral Home.Maria was born on October 21, 1966, at La Barranca Matamoros, Tamaulipas Mexico, to Jesús Iracheta and Rosa María García.  She was preceded in death by her father.She was happily married to Jose Cardona, who was her partner in life for 38 years at the time of her passing.Maria had a bright, energetic personality and was known for being a Jack of All Trades. She touched many lives with her generosity and passion for life. Maria was a light to all who knew her. She was a Christian woman and loved her chickens.Maria is survived by her husband; two sons, Jose Cardona Jr. and Joel Cardona; one daughter-in-law, Brianna Cardona; and one granddaughter, Esmeralda Grace Cardona. She is also survived by one grandson, Lucca Joel Cardona;  eight sisters; and one brother: Blanca Estela Porras, Rosario Hernandez, María Perez, Noemi Dominguez, Sofía Dominguez, Alejandro Dominguez, Elizabeth Martínez, Maricela Ramos and Araceli Castro. She also leaves behind many nieces and nephews.Pallbearers will be; Jose Cardona, Jose Cardona Jr., Joel Cardona, Johnathan Cardona, Alejandro Dominguez and Daniel Strahl.Maria Cardona Memorial VideoPlease join us in remembering Maria by signing our guestbookat fossfunealhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofMaria Lourdes Cardona, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofMaria Lourdes Cardona, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.SERVICESVisitationMonday, July 28, 2025Funeral Home,Get Directions on Google MapsVisitationTuesday, July 29, 202510:00 AM - 11:00 AMValley Mills First Baptist ChurchValley Mills, Texas 76689Get Directions on Google MapsFuneral ServiceTuesday, July 29, 202511:00 AMValley Mills First Baptist ChurchValley Mills, Texas 76689Get Directions on Google MapsPrint ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_4.name: Mary French Keltner
obituary_4.obituary_text: Mary French KeltnerMarch 22, 1940 - May 26, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Mary (French) KeltnerMarch 22, 1940 ~ May 26, 2025Mary Ann Keltner, 85, passed away peacefully at her home on Monday, May 26. 2025. entering eternal rest with her Lord and Savior and reuniting with her beloved family who passed before her.Mary was born on March 22, 1940, at Mother Neff Park to Andy French and Birdie Raborn French. She began her long and dedicated career with Crawford ISD on October 1, 1979 and retired on May 16, 2008 just shy of completing 30 years of service.In her earlier years, Mary enjoyed gardening, farming and hunting. Her greatest joy however was her family. She loved hearing endless stories of her grandchildren and would end every phone conversation with, “give them all hugs and tell them how much their granny loves them”.A devout Baptist Christian, Mary found comfort in her faith. When she could no longer attend church in Valley Mills, she devoted her Sunday mornings to watching First Baptist Church of Waco on television.She was also a talented musician, teaching choir at a young age in church, teaching Sunday school and playing the piano. But most of all were the many cherished moments spent with her siblings, singing and playing the guitar among many other instruments.During her tenure, Mary developed a deep admiration for President George W. Bush, often sharing heartfelt stories of the President, First Lady Laura Bush and members of the press during their visits to the school.Mary was preceded in death by her husband, J.W. Keltner, her son, and Robert Carl Olson.  Also her brothers and sisters:  Billy, A.L., Elmer, Sammy, Jerry, Bonnie, Shirley, and Juanita.She is lovingly remembered by her devoted daughter Lori Roedler-Johnson, and her husband, Charles Johnson; stepdaughter Gwen Keltner Kitchens and husband William F Kitchens (2 Children & 3 Grandchildren), stepsons Richard Duane Keltner and wife Neva (6 Children & 10 Grandchildren) and David C Keltner (7 Children & 13 Grandchildren), her granddaughter, Amber Sutherland and husband Jeff; grandsons Brandon Roedler and wife, Monica, and Jeff Olson and wife, Desiree; and nine additional great-grandchildren who were the light of her life.In honor of Mary’s memory flowers may be sent to Foss Funeral Home or in lieu of flowers, donations can be made to the George Bush Presidential Library - a tribute to the former President she held in such high regard.Mary Keltner Memorial VideoA memorial service and graveside will be held 11 AM Saturday, June 7, at Eagle Springs Cemetery under the direction of Foss Funeral Home & Cremation Center.A reception will follow the service at the Cemetery.The family invites you to sign the online guestbook at www.fossfuneralhome.com or in person at the funeral home.To send a flower arrangement in memory ofMary French Keltner, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofMary French Keltner, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.Print ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_5.name: Tony Valero Felan
obituary_5.obituary_text: Tony Valero FelanMay 10, 1950 - July 11, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|Send Sympathy Card|Tony Valero FelanMay 10, 1950 ~ July 11, 2025Tony Valero Felan, of Morgan, Texas, passed away on July 11th, after a full and blessed life. She was born on May 10, 1950, in the fields by a midwife near Hillsboro, Texas, to Manuel Valero and Petra Eva Villarreal.On April 9, 1967, she married her forever love, Margarito Garza Felan, who never left her side for 46 years—until our Heavenly Father guided him to Heaven on July 1, 2013. They always enjoyed life and getting into trouble together.Always helping family or neighbors in need whenever she could, she would also be helpful to total strangers, friends, and anyone who crossed her path. She had that special spirit that welcomed everyone and never met a stranger. Always extending a hand to neighbors, she would pray for everyone.She is survived by 3 sons and 1 daughter: Rudy Valero Felan and wife, Christina of Meridian, Texas, and their 3 children: Devon, Raven, and Alvin. Jay Joseph Felan and wife, Shelia (deceased) of Georgia, with 3 sons: Larry J. Garrett, Chris Sheppard and Terail J. Sheppard. Margarito Christopher Felan of Mamou, Louisiana. Wife and children are unknown. Melissa Felan Ibarra and husband, Juan Jose Ibarra Sr. of Morgan, Texas, and their 5 children: Maria Luisa, Elena Eva, Juan Jose Ibarra Jr, Victor Antonio, and Margarita Gracella.Tony Valero Felan, you will always be forever in our hearts and fondly remembered. We love you. Goodbye for now—we will see each other on the other side. Until then…Rest in Peace, MommaA private memorial service will be had at a later date by the family.Please join us in remembering Tony by signing our guestbook at fossfuneralhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofTony Valero Felan, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofTony Valero Felan, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.Print ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_6.name: Samuel Don French
obituary_6.obituary_text: Samuel Don FrenchJanuary 26, 1980 - May 9, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Samuel Don FrenchJanuary 26, 1980 ~ May 9, 2025Samuel Don French, actor, writer, producer, age 45, formerly of Clifton, Texas, went to be with the Lord on May 9, 2025.Samuel Don French born January 26,1980, in Waco, Texas to Gary Don French and Ruby Darlene Spence.Samuel graduated from Clifton High School in 1998.(From Clifton Record:  French continued doing theater until he was 15, at which point his lovefor sports prevailed.   After high school he played semi-pro and professional indoor football for 11 years.He had stated that he owed a lot of credit to his root of being raised in Clifton as it helped shaped for his biggest role as a professional actor.  He said "I just really wanted to bring some glory back to my hometown where it all started.)  He attended MCC and ACC where he found his passion for theater arts and became an actor, writer and producer. He has been a part of numerous projects such as “Fear the Walking Dead”, “Texas Rising” and his most recent “Killers of the Flower Moon.” He earned an award during his career for Best Supporting Actor in a Feature Film for “Monsters Within.” He was very involved with his community and took part in multiple organizations. At an early age he knew God had blessed him with talent.  From high school, to college, to California, he followed God's blessings.He was survived by his Father, Gary Don French of Clifton, Texas.  Mother, Ruby Darlene Spence of Clifton, Texas, wife, Melinda French of Dale, Texas, four children, Daniel Campos III, Fabian Campos, Christopher Campos, and Madison French. Brother, Andy French and Daniel French, sister, Gina Kettler and numerous nieces and nephews.Samuel French Memorial VideoFuneral Services will be held 1:00 PM, Friday, May 16, 2025, at the First Baptist Church of Valley Mills, Texas.Visitation will be 1 hour prior to the service at the church.Family and friends are asking for donations to help with funeral expense. Please mail check to Foss Funeral Home, PO Box 164, Valley Mills, TexasorFirst National Bank, PO Box 278, Valley Mills, TexasMake check payable to Andy French for Memorial SamuelPallbearers at the service will be Rodney Willis, Armondo Ruiz, Josh McKain, Danny French, Brandon Lilley, and Gene Williams.Please join us in remembering Sammy by signing our guestbook atfossfunealhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofSamuel Don French, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofSamuel Don French, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.SERVICESVisitationFriday, May 16, 202512:00 PM - 1:00 PMFirst Baptist Church302 Ave. CValley Mills, TXGet Directions on Google MapsFuneral ServiceFriday, May 16, 20251:00 PMFirst Baptist Church302 Ave. CValley Mills, TXGet Directions on Google MapsPrint ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_7.name: Frank Richard Ingram II
obituary_7.obituary_text: Frank Richard Ingram IIMarch 1, 1957 - April 24, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Frank R. Ingram IIMarch 1, 1957 ~ April 24, 2025Frank Richard Ingram II, born on March 1, 1957 in Columbus Ohio, passed from this world April 24, 2025 at his home in Bosque County, Texas. Frank lived life full throttle, they didn’t call him Fast Frank for nothing. There was much to live for and he died with no regrets.Frank leaves behind a family that was always his first priority to care for, provide for, and to keep safe, happy and loved: Candace, his wife of 39 years; son, Frank Richard Ingram III and his wife Rachel; daughter, Savanna Mae Ingram Howard and her husband Luke; and his beloved grandchildren, Cole Barton Howard, Sadie Mae Howard and Frank Richard Ingram IV (aka: FJ).  He is also survived by his father, Frank Richard Ingram I and his wife Diane; sisters, Brenda Ingram, Judy Ann Bates and her husband Chris; nephew, Shylo Young and his wife Chanin; and many other step brothers, step sisters, nieces and nephews. Frank is preceded in death by his mother, Helen Thrash.A little known fact about Frank Ingram was that he never graduated high school. He always wanted to be outside, chasing life and he had no time for such incidentals. Highly intelligent, with sharp mind and dry wit, he was always working, always learning. Frank could chase down any problem with a solution. There is little wonder that at the age of 17 Frank hired on as a grunt, beginning a lifelong career as an electrical transmission lineman and completing that career with his last breath as Vice President of Operations with North Houston Pole Line. Frank loved building high line and was extraordinarily good at it. Frank never withheld what he had learned. He leaves behind hundreds of linemen, operators, foremen, general foremen, safety representatives, estimators and project managers who are better for having worked with and for him.Frank wove through every aspect of his life his love for the outdoors. He loved ranching, making hay, running tractors, as well as, any piece of heavy equipment ever made. Every single thing his wife broke, he could fix. Frank was good at that too.His greatest passion was fishing. Catching was great but he always said, “A bad day fishing beats a good day working, any day.” He shared that passion with his family, friends, co-workers and even the many strangers that he would stop to help with a tow, share bait with or locations where the fish were biting...every chance he got.In the winter of 2010, Frank realized a lifetime dream of off shore fishing when he purchased an old Grady White that he worked on tirelessly for three months before taking out with his wife all summer long. He never did get the head to flush and he sold her to buy his dream boat, a 38’ Fountain, aptly named the Knot Obsessed under the auspicious idea of having a working head for long weekends offshore. And, yes, it worked.Frank Ingram was larger than life. But there was one thing he couldn’t fix. Diagnosed with multiple myeloma, doctors gave him two years to live. That was eight years ago. He fought to be with his kids and grand kids. And it was a fight that he faced with grace and determination.If Frank were standing before you right now he would tell you that there’s never enough time....now go take the kids fishing.Frank never wanted a fuss or a service.Gifts may be made, in his memory, to Texas Parks and Wildlife Foundation at www.tpwf.org.To send a flower arrangement in memory ofFrank Richard Ingram II, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofFrank Richard Ingram II, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.Print ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_8.name: RONALD FRANKLIN BLACKWOOD
obituary_8.obituary_text: RONALD FRANKLIN BLACKWOODSeptember 5, 1940 - June 26, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Ronald Franklin Blackwood9/5/1940 - 6/26/2025Ron Blackwood, age 84, of Lorena, Texas passed into the presence of his Savior surrounded by his family on June 26, 2025. He was a legend in the Gospel and Country music industry for his management, promotion, performance, and concert-booking talents, but Ron would tell you that he only truly cared about working with artists who would share the Gospel of Jesus Christ. He was an enthusiastic encourager who loved without holding back and gave generously to anyone in need. Second to his love for the Lord was his devotion to his family.Ron was born in Shenandoah, Iowa and raised in Memphis, Tennessee. As the son of the late RW (founding Blackwood Brothers member) and Elaine Blackwood, his entire life was surrounded by music. He was very proud of his education at Castle Heights Military Academy and service in the Army’s 101st Airborne Paratroopers. He also beamed when he talked about being the 4th Army Welterweight Champion in the boxing ring. After the Army, Ron’s musical roots would lead him to make an indelible mark on this world.During his 61 years in the music industry, Ron worked with great artists like Willie Nelson, Carman, the Blackwood Brothers, Hank Williams, Jr., Billy Walker, and so many more. He and his brother R. W. Blackwood, Jr. performed on the Grand Ole Opry stage in the 1960s. He even had an opportunity to join Elvis’ entourage in the 1970s but chose to stick closer to his Gospel foundations. He loved performing and was an electric Master of Ceremonies in the many theaters that he and his wife, brother, sister-in-law, various family members, and friends owned or headlined in Branson, Missouri, Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, Maggie Valley, North Carolina, and countless concerts all over the country. Ron was transparent about the good and the bad, but he lived life boldly proclaiming the words of the Bill & Gloria Gaither song “He Touched Me,” knowing that it was his relationship with his blessed Savior that cleansed and made “Ole Ronnie whole.”Ron was proceeded in death by his father RW and mother Elaine Blackwood. He is survived by his wife Shelley Layne Blackwood, brother R.W. and sister-in-law Donna Blackwood, sons Neal (Taryn), Ron (Dana), Nathan (Shiloh), Noah (Alex), and Nicolas (Leslie) Blackwood, and daughters Ronda (Desi) Arellano, Alicia (Graham) Glynn, and Brandi (Jeff) Woods; grandchildren Zachary, Matthew, Paige, Dalton, Courtney, Jordan, Ryien, Sage, and Ezekiel, and great-grandchildren Alexis, Ava, Cash, Saylor, and Skye, numerous cousins, nieces, and nephews.Shelley Blackwood & family would like to thank the staff of Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest’s Emergency Department, MICU, 6th floor, and the McClinton Cancer Center.Because of Ron’s far-reaching legacy including many friends and family around the country, two services will be held in his honor.A memorial service will be held at Oak Grove Baptist Church in China Spring, Texas on Saturday July 12, 2025 at 11:00 AM, with a time of visitation prior to the service starting at 10:00.A celebration of his life will take place on August 2, 2025, at 1:00 PM at Faith and Wisdom Church in Branson, Missouri.In lieu of flowers, please consider honoring Ron Blackwood’s memory by contributing to one or both of the following scholarships:McLennan Community College Foundation Nursing Scholarshipsmailed to 1400 College Dr. Waco, TX 76708 or given online athttps://www.mclennan.edu/foundation/Liberty University's School of Music Business Programsmailed to Liberty University Office of Development, 1971 University Boulevard, Lynchburg, VA 24515 (Check should include Ron Blackwood Endowment in memo) or give online athttps://give.liberty.edu/DGUGENFUND/gift-info(Please check “In Memory of” and type Ron Blackwood Endowment)Ron Blackwood Memorial VideoPlease join us in remembering Ron by signing our guestbook atfossfuneralhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofRONALD FRANKLIN BLACKWOOD, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofRONALD FRANKLIN BLACKWOOD, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.SERVICESVisitationSaturday, July 12, 202510:00 AM - 11:00 AMOak Grove Baptist ChurchChina Spring, TexasGet Directions on Google MapsMemorial ServiceSaturday, July 12, 202511:00 AMOak Grove Baptist ChurchChina Spring, TexasGet Directions on Google MapsCelebration of LifeSaturday, August 02, 20251:00 PMFaith and Wisdom ChurchBranson, obituary_9.name: William Earl Allred
obituary_9.obituary_text: William Earl AllredDecember 22, 1944 - May 4, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|William (Bill) Earl AllredDecember 22nd, 1944 – May 4th, 2025With a wink and a nod to a life filled with adventure and joy, we announce the passing of William Earl Allred, who embarked on his final journey on May 4th, 2025, at his cozy home in Valley Mills, Texas. At the age of 80, Bill lived a life full of enthusiasm and cheer.Born on December 22nd, 1944, in sunny California, Bill was the beloved son of Dennis and Alta Mae Allred. He grew up in Haltom City, Texas, where he attended Haltom High School and undoubtedly charmed everyone with his wit and charisma.Bill was a natural-born salesman, always on the lookout for the next great deal. His passion for fixing up old cars was legendary, and he could often be found under the hood of a classic, bringing it back to life. He loved camping, hunting, fishing, horseback riding and spending quality time with his family, creating memories that will be cherished forever.Bill leaves behind his loving spouse Susie Allred, three wonderful children Lana King, Joshua Allred, and Stephanie Smith, seven amazing grandchildren, three adorable great-grandchildren, and his brother Robert Allred and his wife Linda. He is now reunited with his parents and his sister Lila Allred, undoubtedly sharing stories and laughter once again.William Allred Memorial VideoA private graveside was held by the family.  But don't worry, the real party is in June! Join us for a celebration of life where we can all share our favorite Bill stories and toast to a life well-lived. Information will be posted on this web-site as soon as time set.MEMORIAL SERVICE WILL BE HELD AT 2 PM SUNDAY, JUNE 29, 2025, AT THEWOMACK ZION UNITED CHURCH, 1411 FM 219, CLIFTON, TEXASPlease join us in remembering Bill by signing our guestbook atfossfunealhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofWilliam Earl Allred, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofWilliam Earl Allred, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.Print ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_10.name: Morris Earl Henderson
obituary_10.obituary_text: Morris Earl HendersonJuly 24, 1954 - June 26, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Morris E. HendersonJuly 24, 1954 ~ June 26, 2025Morris Earl Henderson, age 70, passed away on June 26, 2025, in Valley Mills, Texas.  The family will have a private graveside at a later date.  In lieu of flowers  memorials may be made in his memory to Valley Mills Cemetery, PO Box 253, Valley Mills, Texas 76689.Morris was born on July 24, 1954, in Waco, Texas, to James and Evelyn (Morris) Henderson.  The family moved to Pecos then later to Abilene where he attended school.  He spent his career working at Cat Holt. He was preceded in death by his parents and a brother James Henderson Jr.He is survived by his daughter, Micah Prine and her husband Justin;  his son, Matthew Henderson and wife Dannielle; grandchildren Nikolas, Anthony, Lucas, Kaylee; a sister Martha J.Henderson, and nephew James A. Henderson III.Please join us in remembering Morris by signing our guestbook at fossfuneralhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofMorris Earl Henderson, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofMorris Earl Henderson, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.Print ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry

obituary_11.name: Roy Allen Cooper
obituary_11.obituary_text: Roy Allen CooperJuly 16, 1960 - June 4, 2025Share this obituarySend FlowersUpload Photo|Sign Guestbook|View Guestbook Entries|Send Sympathy Card|Roy Allen CooperJuly 16, 1960-June 4, 2025Roy Cooper, age 65. passed away on June 4, 2025, in Amarillo, Texas.Roy Allen Cooper was born on July 16 1960 in Houston, Texas to Joe Arnold Cooper and Margaret Helen (Sivils) Cooper. He was raised in Waco, Texas, but spent the majority of his life in Laguna Park, Texas.  Forty years ago this month Roy was in an automobile accident in Laguna Park which left him disable the rest of his life.Roy had a deep love for classic rock and roll, especially bands like Deep Purple and Grand Funk Railroad, and his music was always playing wherever he was. Though he never had children of his own, Roy had a heart full of love for his many nieces and nephews, whom he loved as if they were his own. To him family was everything. He is preceded in death by his loving mother, Margaret Helen Cooper; and his twin sister Rhonda Hellen Cooper, with whom he shared a special bond.Roy is survived by his siblings: Joe Paul Cooper of Laguna Park, Texas, Gary Lynn Cooper of Waco, Texas, and Michelle Eugenia Cooper of Panama City, Florida. Along with many extended family members and friends.The family will have a private memorial service at a later date.  Roy will be deeply missed, but his memory will live on in the hearts of all who knew him.Please join us in remembering Roy by signing our guestbook atfossfunealhome.comTo send a flower arrangement in memory ofRoy Allen Cooper, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.To plant Memorial Trees in memory ofRoy Allen Cooper, pleaseclick here to visit our sympathy store.Print ObituarySign GuestbookNameEmailMessage500words remainingPlease keep my message privateMoreLessGuestbook OptionsImageLight A CandleCandle 1Candle 2Candle 3Candle 4Required Confirmation:Submit Guestbook entry
